model.label: Kimi K2.5
model.description: Moonshot AI • Reasoning model from the Kimi family built for complex thinking tasks.

pick.rationale: Purdue holds a narrow but decisive statistical advantage with a +30.5 adjusted efficiency margin that exceeds Gonzaga's +28.16 mark, driven by the nation's top-ranked offense that creates matchup problems for any defense. While Gonzaga's elite 9th-ranked defense provides a formidable challenge and the Bulldogs have thrived despite losing Braden Huff to a season-long knee injury, Purdue's Big Ten Tournament championship pedigree and superior offensive firepower provide a thin edge in this tightly contested 2/3 matchup. The Boilermakers' ability to score at an elite level against high-major competition, combined with their 3-spot ranking advantage in both KenPom and Torvik, makes them the slight favorite to advance to the Elite Eight.

reasoningStep.title: Gonzaga vs Purdue Sweet 16 Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Analyzed the 2/3 seed matchup between Purdue and Gonzaga, focusing on the strength-on-strength battle of Purdue's #1 offense against Gonzaga's #9 defense. While both teams are elite, Purdue's ~2.3 point efficiency margin advantage, top-ranked offensive attack, and Big Ten championship momentum provide a narrow edge over the WCC champion Bulldogs who have played without injured star Braden Huff all season.
evidence: Purdue: KenPom/Torvik #8, +30.5 Adj EM, #1 Offense, #36 Defense, 26-8 record, Big Ten champions
Gonzaga: KenPom/Torvik #11, +28.16 Adj EM, #30 Offense, #9 Defense, 30-3 record, WCC champions
Braden Huff (Gonzaga) out since mid-January with knee injury, unlikely to return
Purdue at full strength after winning Big Ten Tournament (4 games in 4 days)

Gonzaga’s NCAA Tournament path only grows more treacherous the longer Braden Huff sits out | Dave Boling March 15, 2026 Updated Mon., March 16, 2026 at 3:44 p.m. X Email Reddit SMS By Dave Boling The Spokesman-Review My on-site scouting report on Gonzaga’s Braden Huff last week: Despite a stubborn knee injury suffered in January, Huff was still able to elevate and was dangerous at the rim. Visual evidence. Following the West Coast Conference Tournament title game, Huff was able to climb up a ladder and snip off a portion of the net at the rim with a pair of scissors. Beyond that? He was still unable to suit up and actually play. The GU star forward’s injured leg has already pushed past its estimated range of repair of between four and eight weeks. Entering the ninth week of recovery, Huff has been ruled out for the opening weekend of the tournament, starting Thursday at Portland’s Moda Center against No. 14-seed Kennesaw State . Their No. 3 seed, and opening weekend venue in Portland, is news that couldn’t be better for GU. But Huff’s absence affects the Zags’ future, and his doubt creates a bittersweet aftertaste during this time when celebration is the mode of the moment. Bitter? Obvious. Wondering what t

You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.

Gonzaga’s NCAA Tournament path only grows more treacherous the longer Braden Huff sits out | Dave Boling March 15, 2026 Updated Mon., March 16, 2026 at 3:44 p.m. X Email Reddit SMS By Dave Boling The Spokesman-Review My on-site scouting report on Gonzaga’s Braden Huff last week: Despite a stubborn knee injury suffered in January, Huff was still able to elevate and was dangerous at the rim. Visual evidence. Following the West Coast Conference Tournament title game, Huff was able to climb up a ladder and snip off a portion of the net at the rim with a pair of scissors. Beyond that? He was still unable to suit up and actually play. The GU star forward’s injured leg has already pushed past its estimated range of repair of between four and eight weeks. Entering the ninth week of recovery, Huff has been ruled out for the opening weekend of the tournament, starting Thursday at Portland’s Moda Center against No. 14-seed Kennesaw State . Their No. 3 seed, and opening weekend venue in Portland, is news that couldn’t be better for GU. Now I have enough information to make an informed pick. Let me summarize the key facts:

1. **Ratings Comparison:**
- Purdue: KenPom #8, Torvik #8, Adj EM +30.5, Offense #2, Defense #36
- Gonzaga: KenPom #11, Torvik #11, Adj EM +28.16, Offense #30, Defense #9

2. **Key Factors:**
- Purdue has the #1/#2 ranked offense in the nation
- Gonzaga has the #9 ranked defense, which is elite
- Purdue's defense is #36, which is good but not elite
- Gonzaga's offense is #30/#37, which is very good but not elite
- Both teams are relatively healthy now - Purdue is at full strength, Gonzaga has been without Braden Huff since mid-January but has dominated without him (30-3 record)

3. **Prior Context:**
- Gonzaga beat BYU in the Round of 32 after BYU lost star Richie Saunders to ACL tear
- Purdue beat Miami in the Round of 32

4. **Matchup Analysis:**
- This is a strength-on-strength matchup: Purdue's elite offense vs Gonzaga's elite defense
- Purdue has a ~2.3 point efficiency margin advantage (+30.5 vs +28.16)
- Purdue ranks about 3 spots higher in both KenPom and Torvik
- Gonzaga has the better defense but worse offense
- Purdue has the best offense but worse defense

5. **Historical Context:**
- This is a 2-seed (Purdue) vs 3-seed (Gonzaga) matchup
- Sweet 16 games between 2 and 3 seeds are typically very close
- Purdue is a 2-seed with top-8 national metrics
- Gonzaga is a 3-seed with top-11 national metrics

6. **Intangibles:**
- Gonzaga has been playing without Huff all season and has gelled well
- Purdue won the Big Ten Tournament (4 games in 4 days)
- Purdue has the #1 offense in the country
- The defensive advantage for Gonzaga might be more valuable in tournament play
 
Purdue's offensive firepower gives them a narrow but meaningful edge in this tight matchup. The Boilermakers' top-ranked offense and slightly superior efficiency metrics make them the pick, despite Gonzaga's stout defensive capabilities.

Based on my analysis of the Gonzaga vs Purdue Sweet 16 matchup, I have sufficient information to make a prediction.

**Key Findings:**

**Statistical Profile:**
- **Purdue**: KenPom/Torvik #8, +30.5 Adj EM, #1/#2 Offense, #36 Defense, 26-8 record
- **Gonzaga**: KenPom/Torvik #11, +28.16 Adj EM, #30 Offense, #9 Defense, 30-3 record

**Health Status:**
- Gonzaga has played without star forward Braden Huff since mid-January due to a knee injury; Mark Few has been "less than optimistic" about his return for the tournament. However, the Bulldogs have dominated without him (30-3 record, WCC champions).
- Purdue is at full strength after winning the Big Ten Tournament (4 games in 4 days).

**Matchup Analysis:**
This is a classic strength-on-strength battle: Purdue's historically elite offense (#1 nationally) against Gonzaga's elite defense (#9 nationally). Purdue holds a ~2.3 point efficiency margin advantage and ranks approximately 3 spots higher in advanced metrics. The Boilermakers' Big Ten-tested resume and championship momentum, combined with their overwhelming offensive firepower, provide a narrow but reliable edge against a Gonzaga squad that has feasted on WCC competition. While the Bulldogs' defense is formidable and this projects as a tightly contested Sweet 16 game, Purdue's ability to score against anyone and their slightly superior overall efficiency profile tilt this matchup toward the 2-seed.
